testSteps.description: When I get category questions for the Salesforce Administrator exam Automation category

restQuery.responseBody:
[
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "The flow deactivated the rule temporarily",
            "B": "Before-save flows bypass validation rules",
            "C": "Before-save flows execute before custom validation rules in the save order and can change the values validation evaluates",
            "D": "Validation rules run before all flows"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "C"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "In the order of execution, before-save record-triggered flows run before custom validation rules, but validation still evaluates the final values.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "Owen is the sales operations analyst at Stonebridge Legal. The solution should minimize long-term maintenance. A before-save flow appears to prevent a validation error by changing a field before the rule evaluates. What explains this?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_680"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Recall action",
            "B": "Final Rejection Actions only",
            "C": "Step rejection behavior: Go back to the previous step",
            "D": "A validation rule on approval history",
            "E": "Outbound message"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "C"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Approval steps can be configured so rejection goes back to the previous step instead of performing all rejection actions.",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "While testing a new rollout at Metro Energy, Aisha discovers a configuration gap. The solution should minimize long-term maintenance. An approval request rejected at step 3 should return to step 2 rather than end the process. Which setting should be used?",
        "category": "Automation",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_143"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "IMAGE()",
            "B": "HYPERLINK()",
            "C": "BEGINS()",
            "D": "ISNEW()",
            "E": "PRIORVALUE()"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "E"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "PRIORVALUE returns the field's prior value during an update, useful for transition logic.",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "During a configuration review at Atlas Health, Keira, the CRM administrator, is asked to make a change. The existing configuration should be reused where possible. A validation rule should block moving Status backward from Approved to Draft. Which function helps compare the previous value?",
        "category": "Automation",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_189"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "HYPERLINK()",
            "B": "VLOOKUP()",
            "C": "IMAGE()",
            "D": "PRIORVALUE()"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "D"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "PRIORVALUE() compares a field's previous value to its new value in validation logic, making it useful for detecting prohibited transitions. VLOOKUP checks values in a custom object, while IMAGE and HYPERLINK are display functions.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "The operations team at Canyon Digital Digital Division wants a validation rule to prevent users from changing a closed Opportunity back to an open stage after it has been marked Closed Won or Closed Lost. Which formula function is most useful for detecting that the prior stage was closed?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_817"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "After-delete record-triggered flow",
            "B": "Scheduled Flow",
            "C": "Approval Process",
            "D": "Screen Flow",
            "E": "Record-triggered flow that runs before delete"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "E"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Before-delete flows can access the record data and block deletion via errors/fault handling. There is no after-delete record-triggered flow option.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "During a configuration review at Ursa Major Solar, Jonah, the CRM administrator, is asked to make a change. The requirement must respect normal Salesforce security behavior. A record must be archived before it is deleted, and deletion should stop if archive creation fails. Which flow trigger should be used?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_344"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "REGEX()",
            "B": "BEGINS()",
            "C": "VLOOKUP()",
            "D": "CONTAINS()",
            "E": "INCLUDES()"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "A"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "REGEX validates a text value against a specific pattern.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "A department at Northstar Labs reports a problem to Victor, the CRM administrator. The business wants the most standard declarative solution. A tracking ID must match two uppercase letters, a hyphen, and six digits. Which validation function is best?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_447"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Copy the elements into every flow",
            "B": "Autolaunched Flow called as a Subflow",
            "C": "Screen Flow with hidden fields",
            "D": "Separate validation rules for each object"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "B"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Reusable logic belongs in an autolaunched flow invoked by parent flows as a subflow.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "After a process change at Cloud Kicks, Victor needs to choose the most maintainable Salesforce configuration. The change should not introduce unnecessary custom objects or Apex. Multiple flows need the same tax calculation logic and the logic should be updated in one place. Which architecture is best?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_642"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Email Alert",
            "B": "Field Update",
            "C": "Send Custom Notification directly",
            "D": "Call Apex directly",
            "E": "Create Record"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "A",
            "B"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Standard approval actions include field updates and email alerts; record creation or direct Apex invocation is not a standard approval action.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "A department at Nimbus Telecom reports a problem to Zoe, the Salesforce administrator. The change should not introduce unnecessary custom objects or Apex. An approval process should update Status and send a standardized candidate email when finally rejected. Which two final rejection actions apply? (Select Two)",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_441"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Autolaunched Flow called as a Subflow",
            "B": "Separate validation rules for each object",
            "C": "Screen Flow with hidden fields",
            "D": "Copy the elements into every flow"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "A"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Reusable logic belongs in an autolaunched flow invoked by parent flows as a subflow.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "Olivia is the operations manager at Cloud Kicks. The existing configuration should be reused where possible. Multiple flows need the same tax calculation logic and the logic should be updated in one place. Which architecture is best?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_576"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "App Manager Utility Item",
            "B": "Report bucket",
            "C": "Campaign Member Status",
            "D": "Add Error element/custom error in the record-triggered flow",
            "E": "Dashboard filter"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "D"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Modern record-triggered flows can surface custom errors to prevent a record save when criteria fail.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "While testing a new rollout at Cobalt Security, Olivia discovers a configuration gap. The existing configuration should be reused where possible. An administrator wants to prevent a save with a custom error directly from a record-triggered flow when criteria are not met. Which Flow capability can be used?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_492"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Field Update",
            "B": "Create Record",
            "C": "Call Apex directly",
            "D": "Email Alert",
            "E": "Launch Screen Flow"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "A",
            "D"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Approval actions support Email Alert, Field Update, Task, and Outbound Message.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "During a configuration review at Bluewave Insurance, Uma, the business systems administrator, is asked to make a change. The business wants the most standard declarative solution. Which two actions can be configured directly as approval process initial/final actions? (Select Two)",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_431"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "After-save path for actions and related records",
            "B": "Validation rule with an email template",
            "C": "Before-save path for fast field updates",
            "D": "Formula field on Account"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "A"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Creating related records and sending emails require an after-save flow because the triggering record must already be saved and available for actions. Before-save flows are for changing fields on the triggering record only.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "A flow at MetroLink Utilities Operations Division must create a related Audit Log record and send an email whenever a high-risk Account is created. The Account ID must already exist before the related record is created. Which path should the administrator use?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_822"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Screen flow launched from a quick action",
            "B": "Scheduled flow running every hour",
            "C": "After-save flow because all record-triggered flows require committed records",
            "D": "Before-save flow for fast field updates"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "D"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Before-save flows are optimized for updating fields on the triggering record before it is committed. After-save flows are for actions and related records, scheduled flows run independently of the save event, and screen flows require user interaction.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "A record-triggered flow at Aurora Devices Transformation Division updates the Account Rating based on Annual Revenue whenever an Account is saved. The flow only changes fields on the triggering Account and should run as efficiently as possible. Which flow path should be used?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_845"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "REGEX()",
            "B": "PRIORVALUE()",
            "C": "VLOOKUP()",
            "D": "ISNEW()",
            "E": "ISCHANGED()"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "D"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "ISNEW() identifies records being created. ISCHANGED and PRIORVALUE are primarily about edits/previous values.",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "After a process change at Bluewave Insurance, Ben needs to choose the most maintainable Salesforce configuration. The business wants the most standard declarative solution. A validation rule should require Reason only when the record is first created. Which function is essential?",
        "category": "Automation",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_148"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Queue",
            "B": "Permission Set Group",
            "C": "Related User",
            "D": "Public Group",
            "E": "Profile"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "A",
            "C",
            "D"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Approval steps can assign to users, related users, queues, roles, or public groups depending setup; profiles and permission set groups are permission constructs, not approvers.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "During a configuration review at Helios Medical, Olivia, the system administrator, is asked to make a change. The business wants the most standard declarative solution. Which three approver target types can be assigned directly in approval steps? (Select Three)",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_336"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Profile",
            "B": "Permission Set Group",
            "C": "Queue",
            "D": "Related User",
            "E": "Public Group"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "C",
            "D",
            "E"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Approval steps can assign to users, related users, queues, roles, or public groups depending setup; profiles and permission set groups are permission constructs, not approvers.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "While testing a new rollout at Universal Containers, Hannah discovers a configuration gap. The change should not introduce unnecessary custom objects or Apex. Which three approver target types can be assigned directly in approval steps? (Select Three)",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_646"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "VLOOKUP()",
            "B": "REGEX()",
            "C": "BEGINS()",
            "D": "INCLUDES()",
            "E": "CONTAINS()"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "B"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "REGEX validates a text value against a specific pattern.",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "A department at Crescent Financial reports a problem to Keira, the platform administrator. The requirement must respect normal Salesforce security behavior. A tracking ID must match two uppercase letters, a hyphen, and six digits. Which validation function is best?",
        "category": "Automation",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_149"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "A new permission set group for automation",
            "B": "Default Workflow User in Process Automation Settings",
            "C": "Modify All Data on the inactive user",
            "D": "A report subscription running user"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "B"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "The Default Workflow User provides a fallback context for time-dependent workflow and scheduled automation when the original user is invalid. Granting permissions to an inactive user does not solve the problem, and report running users do not control flow scheduled paths.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
